global_context.scene_description: A cinematic editorial overhead flat-lay shot on a dark walnut wood surface. Hero subject: a stack of FIVE yearly desk calendars laid out in an overlapping fanned arrangement. Each calendar has a clearly-visible large year label on its cover or top edge: '2022', '2023', '2024', '2025', '2026'. The first four calendars (2022 through 2025) each have a small bright yellow sticky note stuck to them with hand-scrawled black marker text. The sticky notes read in scrawled handwriting: '2022: I'll do it next year', '2023: AC still works', '2024: Maybe after tax season', '2025: One more summer'. The 2026 calendar is the topmost and is OPEN to a specific month page (May), with a single Tuesday date clearly circled in green ink, and next to it a small clean printed receipt or quote from ASH visible showing '$9,500' and 'ONE DAY' and '0% APR'. Single soft theatrical top-light catches the calendars and ASH quote, dramatic shadow falloff to near-black walnut grain at the edges of the frame. Small headline overlaid at the top of the frame in clean bold uppercase white sans-serif: 'FOUR YEARS OF DREAD. ONE DAY OF ACTUAL WORK.' Small italic caption at bottom in white: 'New AC install. $9,500. 0% APR. Price match. Done.' Mood: cold mathematical correction of years of procrastination. Editorial document/object still life with theatrical lighting, NOT documentary photo, NOT cartoon.
